title: "dual heat with GTX 260"
date: 2009-03-10
forum: x86 64-bit Users

### Post by diamondnular on 2009-03-10
Hi folks,

Here is my system specs:
CPU: Q9300
GPU: EVGA GTX 260
OS: Ubuntu Intrepid 64 bit
Driver: Nvidia 177.82

I want to setup dual head (two separate X): one with my Acer 22" (max resolution 1680x1050) monitor, and one with the old Phillips TV 27" (max resolution 800x600). Here is the xorg.conf:

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Mon Nov  3 08:46:04 UTC 2008

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" 3000 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Acer"
    ModelName      "Acer X223W c"
    #HorizSync       30.0 - 80.0
    #VertRefresh     56.0 - 85.0
    #ModeLine       "1280x1024" 135.0 1280 1304 1448 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
    #Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: builtin, VertRefresh source: builtin
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Phillips"
    ModelName      "Phillips 29PT5506"
    #HorizSync       28.0 - 55.0
    #VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    #DisplaySize     300    230
    #HorizSync       28.0 - 60.0
    #VertRefresh     60
    #Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GTX 260"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GTX 260"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    Option         "Coolbits" "1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 1680x1050_60_0 +0+0; CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Device1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    Option         "Coolbits" "1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "TV: 800x600 +0+0; TV: 640x480 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Everything seems to be fine except ONE BIG problem with the TV output: the second destkop on TV is out-range of the TV screen. I can see, like a portion of its only, I can see a little of the menu bar on top. Printscreen does give the full desktop. It looks like the desktop has a higher resolution than the TV. Nvidia-settings and xorg.0.log show that the virtual size of TV is 800x600. Using Nvidia-settings to change resolution of TV does not help anything, it shows a bigger desktop with the same problem: out of range of TV.

The funny thing is with the same xorg.conf, it runs just fine with a different PC: Nvidia FX 5500 and Ubuntu 8.04. TV shows full desktop.

What problem can it be: intrepid, 64 bit, or nvidia driver???

Anybody has any clues?

Thanks,

D.

### Post by diamondnular on 2009-03-10
**Update:** installed newest driver (180.37) but the same problem. I guess it could be either intrepid 8.10 or 64 bit version of ubuntu?

D.

---

### Post by diamondnular on 2009-03-11
**Update:** I believe this is a problem of Virtual Screen size, since I found some others having similar issues ([http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=209474](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=209474) or [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=379034](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=379034)). 

Unfortunately adding Virtual to Screen does not work out for me. Virtual Size always goes to the available modes that I have in xorg.conf. For example, if Virtual is 800 600 and Modes is "800x600" then Virtual is set 800 600. If Virtual is 800 600 and Modes is "1024x768" "800x600" then 1024x768 will be neglected and virtual size goes to 800 600 again.

What exactly controls the desktop size (Modes? Modelines?)? I want to set Desktop Size is bigger than Virtual Screen, can I?

Thanks,

D.

---

### Post by diamondnular on 2009-03-13
**Update:** Both Jaunty Alpha 5 and Alpha 6 have the same issues.

I have been with Linux and Ubuntu for long enough that I do not want windose on my box anymore. But I am really thinking of trying Windose to see if the problem exists.
